Title: Vendor can submit invoice against a closed/expired PO — no validation error shown
Environment: Staging | OS: Windows 11 | Browser: Chrome 120.0 | App Version: 1.0.0-RC2
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Log in as vendor_test@testco.com | 2. Navigate to Invoice Submission | 3. Enter PO: PO-2023-EXPIRED (status: Closed in DB) | 4. System fetches PO without warning | 5. Fill invoice and click Submit
Expected Result: System should display error: "PO-2023-EXPIRED is closed and cannot accept new invoices." Submission should be blocked.
Actual Result: DEFECT: System accepts the invoice against expired PO without error. Invoice record created in DB with status=SUBMITTED.
Impact: Critical financial risk. Vendors can submit invoices against closed POs. AP team may process payments against expired orders. Audit non-compliance risk.
Test Data: PO: PO-2023-EXPIRED (status=CLOSED confirmed via DB query)
Attachments: Screenshot: successful submission toast; DB export showing invoice record linked to closed PO
Workaround: None — do not submit invoices against POs ending in -EXPIRED until fix deployed.

Title: AP rejection email to vendor does not include the rejection reason
Environment: Staging | Email Service: SendGrid (test mode) | App Version: 1.0.0-RC2
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Log in as ap_reviewer@company.com | 2. Open invoice in Submitted status | 3. Click Reject | 4. Enter reason: "Invoice amount does not match PO line item" | 5. Confirm Rejection | 6. Check vendor email
Expected Result: Rejection email should include invoice number, rejection reason, and AP contact for queries.
Actual Result: DEFECT: Vendor rejection email arrives with blank reason field. Email reads: "Your invoice [INV-TEST-008] has been rejected. Reason: " — reason field empty.
Impact: Vendors raise repeated support queries. Increases support workload. Delays re-submission. Damages vendor trust.
Test Data: AP User: ap_reviewer@company.com | Vendor: vendor_test@testco.com | Invoice: INV-TEST-008 | Reason entered: "Invoice amount does not match PO line item"
Attachments: Screenshot: email body with blank reason; API log confirming rejection_reason correctly stored in DB — bug is in email template
Workaround: AP Reviewer must call vendor directly to communicate rejection reason until email template is fixed.

Title: Invoice form allows submission with future invoice date (e.g. 2 years ahead)
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Log in as vendor | 2. Navigate to Invoice Submission | 3. Enter Invoice Date: 2026-12-31 (future date) | 4. Fill remaining fields with valid data | 5. Click Submit
Expected Result: System should reject invoice with future date. Error: "Invoice date cannot be in the future."
Actual Result: DEFECT: Invoice with a future date (2026-12-31) is accepted and submitted successfully. No validation error shown.
Impact: Medium impact. Could cause reporting anomalies and incorrect aging analysis in monthly reports. Financial records show future-dated liabilities.
Test Data: Invoice Date tested: 2026-12-31 (2 years in future)
Attachments: Screenshot: successful submission with future invoice date displayed
Workaround: Manually verify invoice dates before AP approval until fix deployed.

Title: Session token remains valid after user logs out (no server-side invalidation)
Steps to Reproduce: 1. Log in as vendor | 2. Copy JWT token from Authorization header (via DevTools) | 3. Click Logout | 4. Use copied token in Postman: GET /api/v1/invoices with Authorization: Bearer <copied_token>
Expected Result: After logout, the token should be invalidated server-side. API should return HTTP 401 Unauthorized.
Actual Result: DEFECT: After logout, the JWT token remains valid. GET /api/v1/invoices returns HTTP 200 with vendor data. Session not invalidated server-side.
Impact: CRITICAL security vulnerability. A stolen or copied token can be used to access vendor data indefinitely after the user believes they have logged out.
Test Data: JWT token from logged-out session | API endpoint: GET /api/v1/invoices
Attachments: API response screenshot showing 200 after logout; JWT decoded showing no expiry revision
Workaround: None — this is a critical security vulnerability with no safe workaround. Do not test on production.
